title: Baryonyx Origin
description: Reaching around 30 feet in length and weighing up to 2.5 tons, this Early Cretaceous hunter was likely semi-aquatic, with a long, narrow snout good for catching fish and adaptations suggesting it spent significant time in water.
body:
# **Baryonyx Origin** The *Baryonyx* was a giant theropod dinosaur, reaching around 30 feet *(9 meters)* in length and weighing up to 2.5 tons. This Early Cretaceous hunter was likely *semi-aquatic*, with a long, narrow snout good for catching fish and adaptations suggesting it spent significant time in water. *Origin* created by ***Chris_The_Big!*** **Pack image by [TitanusPixel55](https://www.deviantart.com/titanuspixel55/art/JP-Logo-Baryonyx-Ver-2-897161045) on Deviant Art:** Also try my [Spinosaurus Origin](https://modrinth.com/datapack/spinosaurus-origin)! ![Baryonyx](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/1b81cb989291ddcb6177a09b42aae81a66339256.png) ## Abilities:
Spoiler

Mighty Claws

You have sharp and powerful claws.

Hydration Buff

You thrive in water. Getting soaked unleashes your full potential, making you stronger.

High Lung Capacity

You only start to suffocate after a minute underwater.

Fins

Your underwater speed is increased

Like Water

When underwater, you do not sink to the ground unless you want to.

Aqua Affinity

You may break blocks as others do on land.

Scotopic Vision

You can see well in the dark.

Big Posture

You tower over the average human.

Long Legs

You can step up one block tall surfaces as if they were slabs.

Pesco-Carnivore

You're a skilled hunter, with a taste for both meat and fish.

Hunger

You have to eat more to survive.

Deadly fire

Fire is one of your major weaknesses.

Blast Weakness

You take double the damage from any kind of explosion.

Useless Equipment

Your claws make it so you can't use shield effectively. You also don't rely on wings for hunting.
This Origin was initially created for the ***Dead & Buried Jam*** over on the Origins Discord Server! I'm posting it here as a stand-alone Datapack and for any bug fixes!
